old_claim.note: Water Management Guilds provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the node is scoped to the 983 Grand Canal pound-lock technology rather than European municipal or guild water administration.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening history section describing the 983 chamber or pound lock built on the Grand Canal.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the first recorded chamber lock as a canal-navigation engineering solution on China's Grand Canal.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge used a medieval water-management institution as if it were a technical prerequisite, but the scoped invention is an engineered navigation chamber.

ontology_before: Canal lock gates depended on water-management guilds, municipal water supply, and sternpost rudders.
ontology_after: Canal lock gates depend on canal networks and prior water-control gates, with institutional water guilds removed.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: The graph no longer routes Chinese canal-lock origins through later municipal water-management institutions.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to European canal-maintenance institutions rather than lock-gate technology.
A source showed the first scoped lock depended on water-management guild organization.
A future governance node for canal maintenance reintroduced guilds with a different dependent scope.
short_reason: Canal locks are navigation infrastructure, not a downstream product of water-management guilds.
